fieldValues.value: contentMatch provides a guaranteed-recall floor via substring containment after NFC + lowercasing

For a contentMatch predicate with normalized query string q, the implementation MUST include every instance whose Text Projection contains at least one segment whose normalized text contains q as a substring (NFC followed by Unicode simple lowercasing, then substring containment test). This is the guaranteed-recall floor — every instance reachable by this rule must appear in the result set.

A recall-floor rule (rather than an exact definition of content matching) permits implementations to layer ranking, stemming, and semantic similarity on top while guaranteeing a minimum that can be tested via a conformance fixture.
